methods.description: Process for collecting sediment cores using multicorer and gravity corer, sectioning, and preparing subsamples for total cell counts and DNA extraction.

steps.description: Sediment samples were taken from the near-surface sediment down to a depth of up to ca. 0.5 mbsf employing a multicorer (MUC).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect near-surface sediment cores using a multicorer

steps.description: Deeper sediments down to 10 mbsf were sampled using a gravity corer (GC).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect deeper sediment cores using a gravity corer

steps.description: The GC cores were quickly cut into 1 m sections onboard.
steps.searchable_summary: Section gravity cores into 1-meter lengths onboard

steps.description: immediately stored at 4˚C.
steps.searchable_summary: Store core sections at 4 degrees Celsius

steps.description: For further section-wise processing within several hours, each 1 m section was subsequently split lengthwise into half-core sections.
steps.searchable_summary: Split core sections lengthwise into half-core sections

steps.description: During sampling, the outer surface of the core was carefully removed to avoid contamination with seawater.
steps.searchable_summary: Remove outer surface of core to avoid contamination

steps.description: Microbiological samples for the quantification of microorganisms were taken with a sterilized 5 mL syringe of which the Leur head was cut off before.
steps.searchable_summary: Take microbiological subsamples using a modified syringe
inputs: Sterilized 5 mL syringe (Leur head cut off)

steps.description: The uncontaminated center of the sediment core was sampled.
steps.searchable_summary: Sample the uncontaminated center of the sediment core

steps.description: For total cell counts, 0.5 mL sediment was fixed in 1 mL of a cold 4% formaldehyde-PBS [phosphate buffered saline (ultra), 150 mM sodium phosphate, 150 mM NaCl, pH 7.2] for 4-15 h at 4-10˚C
steps.searchable_summary: Fix sediment subsample with formaldehyde-PBS for total cell counts
outputs: Fixed sediment sample for cell counting

steps.description: washed twice with cold PBS using a mini-centrifuge (Eppendorf) at 13000 rpm for 10 min each
steps.searchable_summary: Wash fixed sediment samples with PBS using centrifugation

steps.description: and finally stored at −20˚C in 1 mL PBS-ethanol (1:1) in 2 mL vials (Eppendorf)
steps.searchable_summary: Store washed fixed samples at -20 degrees Celsius in PBS-ethanol

steps.description: transported to BGR frozen with dry ice as air-freight
steps.searchable_summary: Transport frozen samples using dry ice via air-freight

steps.description: and stored in BGR at −20˚C.
steps.searchable_summary: Store transported samples at -20 degrees Celsius at destination

steps.description: For DNA extraction and quantification with Q-PCR, several grams of fresh sediment were frozen immediately after sampling at −20˚C in screw capped vials.
steps.searchable_summary: Freeze fresh sediment samples immediately for DNA extraction and Q-PCR
outputs: Frozen sediment samples for DNA extraction

methods.description: Analysis of the quantitative composition of the total (active and inactive) microbial community by Q-PCR of extracted DNA, including DNA extraction and gene quantification.

steps.description: High-molecular-weight DNA was extracted from 0.5 g of a frozen sediment sample
steps.searchable_summary: Extract high-molecular-weight DNA from frozen sediment samples

steps.description: employing a modified Fast DNA Spin Kit for Soil (Bio101) protocol (Webster et al., 2003).
steps.searchable_summary: Use a modified Fast DNA Spin Kit for Soil protocol for DNA extraction
inputs: Modified Fast DNA Spin Kit for Soil (Bio101)
keywords: Fast DNA Spin Kit for Soil

steps.description: Sterilized quartz sand treated in a muffle furnace for organic carbon removal was used as negative control in the extraction procedure.
steps.searchable_summary: Include sterilized quartz sand as a negative control in the DNA extraction

steps.description: Extracted DNA was amplified by Q-PCR using the device ABI Prism 7000 (Applied Biosystems)
steps.searchable_summary: Perform quantitative real-time PCR (Q-PCR) on extracted DNA using ABI Prism 7000

steps.description: and different master mixes from the companies Applied Biosystems, Eurogentec or Invitrogen.
steps.searchable_summary: Use specified commercial master mixes for Q-PCR

steps.description: Each DNA extract was measured in triplicate.
steps.searchable_summary: Run Q-PCR for each DNA extract in triplicate

steps.description: After each Q-PCR, melting curves were measured for SYBR Green ® I assays.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure melting curves after Q-PCR for SYBR Green assays

steps.description: The copy numbers of the 16S rRNA gene were quantified for prokaryotes, Archaea (Takai and Horikoshi, 2000), Bacteria (Nadkarni et al., 2002), Crenarchaeota (Ochsenreiter et al., 2003), the JS-1-and Chloroflexi-related bacteria (Blazejak and Schippers, 2010), and the metal and sulfate-reducing Geobacteraceae (Holmes et al., 2002).
steps.searchable_summary: Quantify copy numbers of specific 16S rRNA genes using Q-PCR data

steps.description: The 18S rRNA gene of Eukarya was determined as previously described (Schippers and Neretin, 2006).
steps.searchable_summary: Quantify copy numbers of the 18S rRNA gene for Eukarya using Q-PCR data

steps.description: Functional genes were quantified as described elsewhere: mcrA (mcrA assay 1, Wilms et al., 2007; mcrA assay 2, Steinberg andRegan, 2009), dsrA (Schippers andNeretin, 2006); aprA , and cbbL for (RuBisCO; Selesi et al., 2007).
steps.searchable_summary: Quantify copy numbers of specific functional genes (mcrA, dsrA, aprA, cbbL) using Q-PCR data
inputs: Protocols (Wilms et al., 2007; Steinberg and Regan, 2009; Schippers and Neretin, 2006; Selesi et al., 2007)
